seoTitle: Add Hover Effects to Power BI Page Navigation Menus
metaDescription: Learn how to add hover effects to Power BI page navigation, making your interactive menus feel more polished and responsive for viewers.
intro: A subtle hover effect can make Power BI navigation feel far more intuitive and professional. This guide covers how to set up hover effects on your page navigation menu items, giving your dashboard a more interactive, engaging feel.

steps.heading: Set up your Power BI page navigation
steps.body: Start with a working page navigation menu in your report, since hover effects build on top of the existing navigation structure.

steps.heading: Add hover effects to menu items
steps.body: Configure the hover state for each navigation item, adjusting visual feedback so it responds when a viewer moves their cursor over it.

steps.heading: Test the hover functionality
steps.body: Click through and hover over each menu item to confirm the effect displays correctly and feels responsive.

steps.heading: Understand the impact on user experience
steps.body: Hover effects make navigation feel more intuitive, helping viewers quickly understand which elements are clickable within your report.

steps.heading: Apply this to real dashboard scenarios
steps.body: A sales dashboard, for example, becomes easier to explore when hovering over menu items visually signals where users can click to see different visualizations.

faq.question: Do hover effects work the same way on touchscreen devices?
faq.answer: Hover effects are primarily designed for mouse-based interaction, so touchscreen devices may not display the same behavior since there's no true hover state on touch.

faq.question: Will adding hover effects slow down my Power BI report?
faq.answer: No, hover effects are a lightweight visual enhancement and don't meaningfully impact your report's performance.

faq.question: Can I customize the color change for a hover effect?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize the visual change that occurs on hover, including color, to match your report's overall design.

faq.question: Is adding hover effects beginner-friendly in Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, the process is approachable for beginners, especially when starting from an existing navigation setup.
